regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, expect easterly winds at 11 knots accompanied by thunderstorms with an 11% rain probability, which should not significantly disrupt port operations or shore excursions. Looking ahead, Port Everglades will experience east winds increasing to 15 knots with overcast skies and a 24% rain probability over the next 24 to 48 hours, which may warrant cautious scheduling of outdoor activities. By the third day, both Miami and Port Everglades will see steady east winds at 15 knots with drizzle and a 24% rain probability, suggesting damp conditions but no major operational impacts. Cruise vessels should prepare for consistent easterly winds and overcast to drizzly conditions across these key Florida ports during this period.
